title: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering
summary: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering retrieves prior skills in an original independent multi-barrier release decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply accessibility disability models, accessibility tree, keyboard focus, accessible names descriptions, aria use, accessible errors status, accessible media equivalents, accessibility test strategy in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent multi-barrier release decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue and harden the Community Needs Survey artifact here. -->

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: extreme-heat service barrier review · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-barrier release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent multi-barrier release decision.

content.title: independent multi-barrier release decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-barrier release decision case 1: accessibility is a niche feature for a small fixed category of users.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 1: trace each task through varied input, perception, comprehension, motion, and environmental conditions, then remove the barrier at its source.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 1: build one separate accessible version after the standard product is complete.

steps.title: 02 · Sequence 2: survey control announcement investigation · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Reconstruct the verification workflow. Scenario: independent multi-barrier release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 2 in independent multi-barrier release decision.

content.text: Order the independent multi-barrier release decision work by dependency and verify that each phase creates evidence needed by the next.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 2: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 2: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 2: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: independent multi-barrier release decision phase 2.1: select a representative interactive element in the DOM
independent multi-barrier release decision phase 2.2: predict its name, role, state, description, and tree position
independent multi-barrier release decision phase 2.3: inspect the computed accessibility properties and rendered exposure
independent multi-barrier release decision phase 2.4: repair the earliest source relationship and confirm the announcement

steps.title: 03 · inspect 3: keyboard-only survey completion route · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 3: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-barrier release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 3 in independent multi-barrier release decision.

content.title: independent multi-barrier release decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 3: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 3: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 3: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-barrier release decision case 3: adding positive tabindex values repairs any keyboard sequence.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 3: keep DOM and visual order aligned, use native controls, preserve visible focus, and test every task without a pointer.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 3: remove focus outlines after mouse use because keyboard users can remember position.

steps.title: 04 · answer 4: ambiguous survey control naming audit · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-barrier release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in independent multi-barrier release decision.

content.title: independent multi-barrier release decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 4: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 4: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 4: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-barrier release decision case 4: adding aria-label improves every element even when visible text already names it.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 4: prefer visible native labels, predict the name-computation result, and add a separate description only when supporting detail is needed.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 4: give all controls the same short name because surrounding layout supplies context.

steps.title: 05 · answer 5: custom survey behavior architecture review · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-barrier release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 5 in independent multi-barrier release decision.

content.title: independent multi-barrier release decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 5: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 5: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 5: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-barrier release decision case 5: aRIA attributes automatically implement keyboard interaction and state changes.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 5: start with native HTML, use the smallest ARIA addition for an actual gap, and verify semantics plus complete behavior over time.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 5: replace every button with a div role button so visual styling is easier.

steps.title: 06 · predict 6: failed survey submission recovery incident · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 6: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-barrier release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 6 in independent multi-barrier release decision.

content.title: independent multi-barrier release decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 6: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 6: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 6: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-barrier release decision case 6: color and a red border give every learner enough error information.

Independent multi-barrier release decision case 6: summarize what failed, connect specific text to each invalid field, move focus deliberately, preserve input, and announce status at the right urgency.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 6: use assertive live regions for every keystroke so no change can be missed.

options.text: independent multi-barrier release decision phase 7.1: inventory speech, meaningful sound, visual-only action, text, and interactive controls
independent multi-barrier release decision phase 7.2: author captions, transcript, and audio-description support for the relevant information
independent multi-barrier release decision phase 7.3: review timing, speakers, terminology, reading order, and control access
independent multi-barrier release decision phase 7.4: complete the same information task with sound off, visuals unavailable, and keyboard only

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 8: identify the user task and data relationship first.

Mastery Exam: Accessible HTML Engineering checkpoint 8: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-barrier release decision case 8: a perfect automated score proves conformance and usable access.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 8: use automation as one evidence source, run task-based manual and assistive checks, document environments, and retest affected paths after repair.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 8: test one browser and viewport because accessibility APIs normalize every environment.

options.text: Independent multi-barrier release decision case 10: screen readers announce exactly the visible text and ignore programmatic semantics.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 10: inspect computed name, role, state, description, and tree position, then trace unexpected output back to source and rendered state.
Independent multi-barrier release decision case 10: add aria-label to every node so DOM semantics no longer matter.
